steps.title: Identify Calder's claim
steps.content: In Text 1, Calder argues that Appia lacked technical expertise in the use of light in the theater.

steps.title: Find Text 2's counter
steps.content: Text 2 directly rejects this by saying Appia was "not an amateur" and points to his apprenticeship with a lighting specialist, where he observed portable lighting devices firsthand. That apprenticeship is the source of his technical expertise.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• "Many lighting technicians dismissed Appia's ideas about light on the stage.": Text 2 never mentions technicians reacting to Appia; this is unsupported overreach. ✗
  • "Appia likely gained a level of technical expertise during his time as an apprentice.": directly mirrors Text 2's argument and rebuts Calder's claim. ✓
  • "Theater practitioners who worked with Appia greatly admired his work.": Text 2 says nothing about admiration from collaborators; off topic. ✗
  • "Appia was unfamiliar with the use of music and sound in theater.": the disagreement is about lighting, not music; introduces an irrelevant topic. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
